session_hint: the Elasticsearch aggregation query slowdown investigation
question: What was the root cause of the Elasticsearch aggregation queries slowing down?
turns: user
Aggregation queries on our search cluster jumped from 200ms p99 to 8 seconds. Happened right after an index mapping update deployed Monday.
user
Checked cluster shard distribution: all data nodes have roughly equal primary and replica shard counts via _cat/shards. No hot shards, no imbalance. Not the issue.
user
Heap usage on all nodes is under 60% of the 30GB JVM heap. GC pauses averaging 80ms per _nodes/stats. Not a JVM memory pressure issue.
user
Tried increasing index.refresh_interval from 1s to 30s to reduce indexing pressure during off-peak. Aggregation query times unchanged. Refresh interval not the cause.
user
Ran the Profile API on a slow aggregation. Fetch phase on the aggregation field takes 97% of query time. Before Monday it was under 10%.
user
Diffed the old and new index mappings. Old mapping had doc_values: true on the category_id field we aggregate on. The new mapping omitted it entirely, so it defaulted to false.
user

Without doc_values, Elasticsearch can't use the columnar BKD structure for aggregations. It falls back to loading fielddata into heap on demand — scanning the entire field from inverted index on every aggregation request.
user
Also found indices.fielddata.cache.size set to '0b' in elasticsearch.yml, effectively disabling the fielddata cache. So every aggregation does a full field scan with no caching benefit.
user

Root cause: the mapping change removed doc_values from the aggregation field, forcing ES to use uncached fielddata loading on every query. Two compounding problems — no doc_values and no fielddata cache — made every aggregation a full field scan.
user
Interim fix: set fielddata cache to 20% of heap. Aggregations drop to 1.8s. Real fix requires reindexing with doc_values re-enabled.
user
Reindex completed after 4 hours. Aggregation queries back to 175ms. Heap pressure also reduced.
user
Shard distribution, JVM GC, and refresh interval were all clean. Added doc_values: true to our index template defaults and a mapping diff check to the deploy pipeline.
